sections.paragraph:
φιλόϋλος, ον (ὕλη ‘material’) loving material things (cp. Origen, Fgm. in Luc. 71, l. 6 ed. MRauer 1930, p. 269 φιλοΰλων καὶ φιλοσωμάτων λόγοι πιθανοί) in imagery πῦρ φιλόϋλον a fire that longs for material things or that desires to be fed w. material things IRo 7:2 (ὕλη means ‘material things’ opposed to God 6:2, elsewh. = ‘firewood’ [ὕλη 2]).—DELG s.v. ὕλη.

  1. cp. = compare, freq. in ref. to citation fr. ancient texts

Fgm. Fgm. = fragment, fragmentary

  1. ed. = edited (by), edition

IRo IRo = Ignatius to the Romans—List 1

DELG DELG = PChantraine, Dictionnaire étymologique de la langue grecque—List 6

s.v. s.v. = sub voce (under the word, look up the word)

Arndt, William ; Danker, Frederick W. ; Bauer, Walter: A Greek-English Lexicon of the New Testament and Other Early Christian Literature. 3rd ed. Chicago : University of Chicago Press, 2000, S. 1060
